topic.what: Early systematic monograph devoted entirely to stomach diseases, establishing clinical-pathological correlation approach.
topic.how: Consolidated mid-19th-century knowledge distinguishing acute from chronic gastritis and linking symptoms to anatomical findings.
topic.why_matters: Established the clinical-pathological correlation framework that remained dominant until endoscopy and histology transformed the field a century later.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether it introduced lasting disease classification concepts
characteristics.value: Acute vs chronic gastritis distinction formalized

novelty: First monograph devoted entirely to stomach diseases
Formalized acute vs chronic gastritis distinction
